Docs
Architecture

Architecture

# Overview

JephyPHP Framework Documentation follows a clean, modular architecture that separates concerns while maintaining high performance and flexibility. This section explains the core architectural patterns and how different components interact to handle HTTP requests.

MVC (Model-View-Controller)

JephyPHP Framework Documentation implements the classic Model-View-Controller pattern with modern enhancements, providing clear separation between business logic, presentation, and request handling.

The Three Components

Component Responsibility Directory
Model Business logic, data access, validation jephy-mvc/app/models/
View Presentation layer, user interface jephy-mvc/app/views/
Controller Request handling, orchestration jephy-mvc/app/controllers/

How MVC Works in Jephy-MVC


php

// 1. Route points to Controller
// jephy-mvc/routes.php
$router->get('/products/{id}', 'ProductController@show');

// 2. Controller receives request
// jephy-mvc/app/controllers/ProductController.php
namespace App\Controllers;

use App\Models\Product;
use Core\Controller;

class ProductController extends Controller {
    public function show($id) {
        // 3. Controller asks Model for data
        $product = Product::find($id);
        $relatedProducts = Product::where('category_id', $product->category_id)->limit(4)->get();
        
        // 4. Controller passes data to View
        $this->view->assign('product', $product);
        $this->view->assign('related', $relatedProducts);
        $this->view->assign('pageTitle', $product->name);
        
        // 5. View renders HTML
        return $this->view->display('products/show.tpl');
    }
}

// 6. Model handles data logic
// jephy-mvc/app/models/Product.php
namespace App\Models;

use Core\Model;

class Product extends Model {
    protected $table = 'products';
    protected $fillable = ['name', 'price', 'description', 'category_id'];
    
    public function getDiscountedPrice() {
        return $this->price * 0.9; // 10% discount logic
    }
}

// 7. View presents data
// jephy-mvc/app/views/products/show.tpl
{extends file="layouts/app.tpl"}

{block name="content"}
    <h1>{$product->name|escape}</h1>
    <p class="price">${$product->price}</p>
    <p class="discount">Special: ${$product->getDiscountedPrice()}</p>
    <div class="description">{$product->description}</div>
    
    <h3>Related Products</h3>
    {foreach $related as $item}
        <a href="/products/{$item->id}">{$item->name}</a>
    {/foreach}
{/block}

MVC Flow Diagram

Browser Request
      │
      ▼
   Router ──────► Middleware Pipeline
      │
      ▼
  Controller ◄─── Request Object
      │
      ├──────► Model ◄───► Database
      │
      └──────► View (Smarty Template)
                   │
                   ▼
              HTTP Response

Best Practices for MVC

Do's:
  • Keep controllers thin (only request handling and orchestration)
  • Put business logic in models
  • Keep views free of PHP logic (use Smarty templates)
  • Use dependency injection in controllers
Don'ts:
  • Don't put database queries directly in views
  • Don't perform complex business logic in controllers
  • Don't echo or print from controllers (always return)
  • Don't access $_POST/$_GET directly (use request object) except you know what you are doing.

Service Container

The Service Container is a powerful tool for managing class dependencies and performing dependency injection. It allows you to register, resolve, and manage services throughout your application.

What is a Service Container?

A Service Container (also known as a Dependency Injection Container) is an object that knows how to create and configure other objects. It manages dependencies and reduces tight coupling between classes.

Core Service Container Implementation

// jephy-mvc/core/Container.php
namespace Core;

class Container {
    private static $instance = null;
    private $bindings = [];
    private $instances = [];
    
    public static function getInstance() {
        if (self::$instance === null) {
            self::$instance = new self();
        }
        return self::$instance;
    }
    
    // Bind an interface/class to a concrete implementation
    public function bind($abstract, $concrete = null, $singleton = false) {
        if ($concrete === null) {
            $concrete = $abstract;
        }
        
        $this->bindings[$abstract] = [
            'concrete' => $concrete,
            'singleton' => $singleton
        ];
    }
    
    // Bind a singleton (one instance for entire application)
    public function singleton($abstract, $concrete = null) {
        $this->bind($abstract, $concrete, true);
    }
    
    // Resolve a class from the container
    public function make($abstract, $parameters = []) {
        // Return existing instance if singleton
        if (isset($this->instances[$abstract])) {
            return $this->instances[$abstract];
        }
        
        // Get binding or use abstract as concrete
        $binding = $this->bindings[$abstract] ?? [
            'concrete' => $abstract,
            'singleton' => false
        ];
        
        $concrete = $binding['concrete'];
        
        // Build the object
        $object = $this->build($concrete, $parameters);
        
        // Store if singleton
        if ($binding['singleton']) {
            $this->instances[$abstract] = $object;
        }
        
        return $object;
    }
    
    // Build an object using reflection
    private function build($class, $parameters = []) {
        $reflection = new \ReflectionClass($class);
        
        if (!$reflection->isInstantiable()) {
            throw new \Exception("Class {$class} is not instantiable");
        }
        
        $constructor = $reflection->getConstructor();
        
        if ($constructor === null) {
            return $reflection->newInstance();
        }
        
        $constructorParams = $constructor->getParameters();
        $dependencies = [];
        
        foreach ($constructorParams as $param) {
            $paramType = $param->getType();
            
            if ($paramType && !$paramType->isBuiltin()) {
                // Resolve dependency from container
                $dependencies[] = $this->make($paramType->getName());
            } elseif (isset($parameters[$param->getName()])) {
                // Use passed parameter
                $dependencies[] = $parameters[$param->getName()];
            } else {
                // Use default value if available
                if ($param->isDefaultValueAvailable()) {
                    $dependencies[] = $param->getDefaultValue();
                } else {
                    throw new \Exception("Cannot resolve dependency {$param->getName()}");
                }
            }
        }
        
        return $reflection->newInstanceArgs($dependencies);
    }
}

Registering Services

Create a service provider or register in bootstrap.php:


// jephy-mvc/bootstrap.php
use Core\Container;

$container = Container::getInstance();

// Bind interfaces to implementations
$container->bind('App\Interfaces\UserRepositoryInterface', 'App\Repositories\DatabaseUserRepository');
$container->bind('App\Interfaces\MailerInterface', 'App\Services\SmtpMailer');

// Register singletons
$container->singleton('App\Services\CacheService', function() {
    return new \App\Services\CacheService('redis');
});

// Bind with custom factory
$container->bind('App\Services\PaymentGateway', function() {
    $config = Config::get('payment');
    return new \App\Services\StripeGateway($config['api_key']);
});

Manual Resolution from Container


php

// Anywhere in your application
$container = \Core\Container::getInstance();

// Resolve a class with dependencies
$userRepo = $container->make('App\Repositories\UserRepository');

// Resolve with custom parameters
$paymentGateway = $container->make('App\Services\StripeGateway', [
    'apiKey' => 'sk_test_12345'
]);

Service Provider Pattern (Optional Advanced)


// jephy-mvc/app/providers/AppServiceProvider.php
namespace App\Providers;

use Core\ServiceProvider;

class AppServiceProvider extends ServiceProvider {
    public function register() {
        // Register services
        $this->container->singleton('App\Services\Logger', function() {
            return new \App\Services\FileLogger(storage_path('logs/app.log'));
        });
    }
    
    public function boot() {
        // Boot services after all registrations
        $logger = $this->container->make('App\Services\Logger');
        $logger->info('Application bootstrapped');
    }
}

Middleware Pipeline

Middleware provides a mechanism for filtering HTTP requests entering your application. It sits between the client request and your controller, allowing you to inspect, modify, or reject requests.

What is Middleware?

Middleware acts as a series of layers that a request must pass through before reaching your controller and a response must pass through before being sent to the client.

Middleware Pipeline Implementation

php

// jephy-mvc/core/MiddlewarePipeline.php
namespace Core;

class MiddlewarePipeline {
    private $middlewares = [];
    private $request;
    
    public function __construct($request) {
        $this->request = $request;
    }
    
    // Add middleware to pipeline
    public function add($middleware) {
        $this->middlewares[] = $middleware;
        return $this;
    }
    
    // Add multiple middlewares
    public function addMany(array $middlewares) {
        foreach ($middlewares as $middleware) {
            $this->add($middleware);
        }
        return $this;
    }
    
    // Execute the pipeline
    public function then($finalCallback) {
        $pipeline = array_reduce(
            array_reverse($this->middlewares),
            function ($next, $middleware) {
                return function ($request) use ($next, $middleware) {
                    return $middleware->handle($request, $next);
                };
            },
            $finalCallback
        );
        
        return $pipeline($this->request);
    }
}

Common Middleware Examples

1. Authentication Middleware

php

// jephy-mvc/app/middleware/AuthMiddleware.php
namespace App\Middleware;

use Core\Middleware;

class AuthMiddleware extends Middleware {
    public function handle($request, $next) {
        // Check if user is logged in
        if (!isset($_SESSION['user_id'])) {
            // Store intended URL for redirect after login
            $_SESSION['intended_url'] = $_SERVER['REQUEST_URI'];
            
            // Redirect to login page
            $this->redirect('/login');
        }
        
        // User is authenticated, proceed
        return $next($request);
    }
}
2. Role-Based Middleware

php

/// jephy-mvc/app/middleware/AdminMiddleware.php
namespace App\Middleware;

use Core\Middleware;
use App\Models\User;

class AdminMiddleware extends Middleware {
    public function handle($request, $next) {
        if (!isset($_SESSION['user_id'])) {
            $this->redirect('/login');
        }
        
        $user = User::find($_SESSION['user_id']);
        
        if ($user->role !== 'admin') {
            $this->abort(403, 'Access denied. Admin privileges required.');
        }
        
        return $next($request);
    }
}
3. Rate Limiting Middleware

php

// jephy-mvc/app/middleware/RateLimitMiddleware.php
namespace App\Middleware;

use Core\Middleware;

class RateLimitMiddleware extends Middleware {
    private $maxRequests = 60;  // requests per minute
    private $decayMinutes = 1;
    
    public function handle($request, $next) {
        $ip = $_SERVER['REMOTE_ADDR'];
        $key = "rate_limit:{$ip}";
        
        if (!isset($_SESSION[$key])) {
            $_SESSION[$key] = [
                'count' => 1,
                'expires_at' => time() + ($this->decayMinutes * 60)
            ];
        } else {
            $data = $_SESSION[$key];
            
            if (time() > $data['expires_at']) {
                // Reset counter
                $_SESSION[$key] = [
                    'count' => 1,
                    'expires_at' => time() + ($this->decayMinutes * 60)
                ];
            } elseif ($data['count'] >= $this->maxRequests) {
                $this->abort(429, 'Too many requests. Please try again later.');
            } else {
                $_SESSION[$key]['count']++;
            }
        }
        
        return $next($request);
    }
}
4. CORS Middleware

php

// jephy-mvc/app/middleware/CorsMiddleware.php
namespace App\Middleware;

use Core\Middleware;

class CorsMiddleware extends Middleware {
    public function handle($request, $next) {
        // Allow from any origin (configure as needed)
        header("Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *");
        header("Access-Control-Allow-Methods: GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, OPTIONS");
        header("Access-Control-Allow-Headers: Content-Type, Authorization, X-Requested-With");
        
        // Handle preflight requests
        if ($_SERVER['REQUEST_METHOD'] === 'OPTIONS') {
            http_response_code(200);
            exit;
        }
        
        return $next($request);
    }
}
5. CSRF Protection Middleware

php

// jephy-mvc/app/middleware/CsrfMiddleware.php
namespace App\Middleware;

use Core\Middleware;

class CsrfMiddleware extends Middleware {
    public function handle($request, $next) {
        $excludedRoutes = ['/api/webhook', '/payment/callback']; // Routes to skip
        
        if (in_array($_SERVER['REQUEST_URI'], $excludedRoutes)) {
            return $next($request);
        }
        
        if ($_SERVER['REQUEST_METHOD'] === 'POST') {
            $token = $request->get('csrf_token') ?? $_SERVER['HTTP_X_CSRF_TOKEN'] ?? null;
            
            if (!isset($_SESSION['csrf_token']) || $token !== $_SESSION['csrf_token']) {
                $this->abort(403, 'CSRF token validation failed.');
            }
        }
        
        return $next($request);
    }
}

Registering Middleware

Global Middleware (runs on every request):

php

// jephy-mvc/bootstrap.php
$app =  new Framework();
$app->addGlobalMiddleware([
    'App\Middleware\CorsMiddleware',
    'App\Middleware\CsrfMiddleware',
]);
Route-Specific Middleware:

php

// jephy-mvc/routes.php
// Single middleware
$router->get('/dashboard', 'DashboardController@index')
       ->middleware('AuthMiddleware');

// Multiple middleware
$router->get('/admin/users', 'AdminController@users')
       ->middleware(['AuthMiddleware', 'AdminMiddleware']);

// Group with middleware
$router->group(['middleware' => ['AuthMiddleware']], function($router) {
    $router->get('/profile', 'ProfileController@index');
    $router->post('/profile/update', 'ProfileController@update');
    $router->get('/orders', 'OrderController@index');
});

Middleware Order of Execution

Middleware executes in the order they are registered:

php

Request → Middleware 1 → Middleware 2 → Middleware 3 → Controller
           ↓ (before)      ↓ (before)      ↓ (before)
           ↓               ↓               ↓
           ↓ (after)       ↓ (after)       ↓ (after)
Response ← Middleware 1 ← Middleware 2 ← Middleware 3
Example with logging:

php

class LogRequestMiddleware extends Middleware {
    public function handle($request, $next) {
        // Before controller (request logging)
        error_log("Request: " . $request->getMethod() . " " . $request->getUri());
        
        $response = $next($request);
        
        // After controller (response logging)
        error_log("Response sent with status code: " . http_response_code());
        
        return $response;
    }
}

Request Life Cycle

Understanding the request life cycle is crucial for debugging and extending Jephy-MVC. This section traces an HTTP request from entry point to response.

Complete Request Life Cycle Flow


1. Entry Point (public/index.php)
   │
   ├─► 2. Load Configuration
   │
   ├─► 3. Bootstrap Framework
   │
   ├─► 4. Service Container Initialization
   │
   ├─► 5. Request Creation
   │
   ├─► 6. Routing
   │
   ├─► 7. Middleware Pipeline
   │
   ├─► 8. Controller Resolution
   │
   ├─► 9. Method Execution
   │    │
   │    ├─► Model Interaction (Database)
   │    │
   │    └─► View Rendering (Smarty)
   │
   ├─► 10. Response Creation
   │
   └─► 11. Response Sending

Detailed Life Cycle Steps

  • Entry Point (public/index.php
  • Bootstrap Framework (jephy-mvc/bootstrap.php)
  • Request Creation
  • Routing
  • Middleware Pipeline & Controller Execution
  • Controller Method Execution

Life Cycle Events & Hooks

JephyPHP Framework Documentation provides hooks to tap into the request life cycle:

php

// jephy-mvc/app/hooks/LifecycleHooks.php
namespace App\Hooks;

class LifecycleHooks {
    
    // Before any request is processed
    public function beforeRequest($request) {
        // Log request start time
        $request->startTime = microtime(true);
    }
    
    // After route is matched but before middleware
    public function onRouteMatched($route, $params) {
        error_log("Route matched: {$route} with params " . json_encode($params));
    }
    
    // Before controller execution
    public function beforeController($controller, $method) {
        error_log("Executing {$controller}@{$method}");
    }
    
    // After controller execution, before response sent
    public function afterController($response) {
        // Add custom headers or modify response
        header('X-Powered-By: Jephy-MVC');
        return $response;
    }
    
    // After response sent
    public function onResponseSent() {
        // Log request completion
        $executionTime = microtime(true) - $_SERVER['REQUEST_TIME_FLOAT'];
        error_log("Request completed in {$executionTime} seconds");
    }
}

Visual Timeline of Request Life Cycle


Time ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────►

[0ms]    Client sends HTTP request
          │
[1ms]    public/index.php (Entry point)
          │
[2ms]    Load config.conf & vendor autoload
          │
[5ms]    Bootstrap framework & container
          │
[8ms]    Create Request object
          │
[10ms]   Router matches URL to controller
          │
[12ms]   Middleware pipeline starts
          │  ├─ Global middleware (CORS, CSRF, etc.)
[15ms]   │  ├─ Route middleware (Auth, RateLimit, etc.)
          │  └─
[20ms]   Controller instantiated (DI applied)
          │
[25ms]   Controller method executes
          │  ├─ Model query (30-50ms if database)
[60ms]   │  ├─ Business logic
[70ms]   │  └─ View rendering with Smarty
[85ms]   │
[90ms]   Response object created
          │
[95ms]   Middleware pipeline (after phase)
          │
[100ms]  Response sent to client
          │
[101ms]  Life cycle complete

Debugging the Life Cycle

Enable debug logging to trace the request life cycle:


// jephy-mvc/config.conf
[app]
site.debug = true

// In bootstrap.php
if (Config::get('app.debug')) {
    $hooks = new \App\Hooks\DebugHooks();
    $hooks->enableProfiling();
}



php

// Debug hook implementation
class DebugHooks {
    private $timeline = [];
    
    public function enableProfiling() {
        $this->timeline['start'] = microtime(true);
        
        // Register hooks
        App\Core\Hook::register('before_request', [$this, 'logBefore']);
        App\Core\Hook::register('after_controller', [$this, 'logAfter']);
        App\Core\Hook::register('response_sent', [$this, 'logSummary']);
    }
    
    public function logBefore() {
        $this->timeline['before_request'] = microtime(true);
    }
    
    public function logAfter() {
        $this->timeline['after_controller'] = microtime(true);
    }
    
    public function logSummary() {
        $timings = [];
        $prev = $this->timeline['start'];
        
        foreach ($this->timeline as $stage => $time) {
            if ($stage !== 'start') {
                $timings[$stage] = round(($time - $prev) * 1000, 2) . 'ms';
                $prev = $time;
            }
        }
        
        echo "";
    }
}

Summary

ON THIS PAGE
